name: PAD - Diagnosis Code Query (Multi-Form)
description: Tests ability to find PAD patients via diagnosis codes. PAD is documented under multiple SNOMED codes by clinical pattern: parent peripheral vascular disease, peripheral arterial occlusive disease, intermittent claudication (characteristic finding), and diabetic-specific peripheral angiopathy variants. Synthea distributes patients across these forms.
prompt: Find all patients with a diagnosis of peripheral arterial disease, including peripheral vascular disease, peripheral arterial occlusive disease, intermittent claudication, and diabetic peripheral angiopathy.

prompts.naive: Find all patients with peripheral arterial disease.
prompts.broad: Find all patients diagnosed with any form of peripheral arterial disease — including peripheral vascular disease (parent), peripheral arterial occlusive disease, intermittent claudication (the characteristic exertional leg pain), and diabetic-specific peripheral angiopathy variants.
prompts.expert: I need to identify all PAD patients. PAD is documented under multiple SNOMED codes from the AIR Atherosclerosis and PAD VSAC: Peripheral vascular disease (400047006) is the broader parent, with Peripheral arterial occlusive disease (399957001), Intermittent claudication finding (63491006) — the symptomatic variant, Peripheral angiopathy due to T2DM (314902007), and Peripheral vascular disorder due to DM (421895002). Search Condition resources for all five codes.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with PAD diagnosis codes. **First peripheral vascular phenotype** (distinct from coronary CHD or venous VTE).

required_codes.display: Peripheral angiopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Peripheral vascular disorder due to diabetes mellitus
